rationale: Keep: the cited 2006 paper is highly aligned with the citing paper’s core mechanism, showing that interval description (extent vs. date) changes discounting via perceived interval length. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior attention/subadditivity and time-description findings with direct subjective-time measurement, which makes this likely more than an incidental background mention. With this close conceptual overlap in temporal discounting and perceived time, it merits full relation judgment.
